title: Schema Field Order â€” Autoregressive Steering
one_liner: Place dependent fields AFTER their dependencies in structured-output schemas; the model generates left-to-right and later fields can use earlier ones.

title: Weak-Model Preselection
one_liner: Run a cheap model first to filter/classify/rank inputs (returning only refs/IDs), then pass the filtered set to the strong model for actual reasoning.

title: File-Reference Passing Between Pipeline Steps
one_liner: Pass references (paths/IDs/URLs), not content, between agent stages â€” the next stage loads on demand. Cuts context cost and improves recoverability.

title: Semantic Field Naming
one_liner: Field names are part of the prompt â€” specific English names trigger pre-trained priors; renaming final_choiceâ†’answer swung accuracy 4.5%â†’95%.

title: Field Descriptions as Inline Mini-Prompts
one_liner: Each Field(description=) is a tiny per-field prompt; precise descriptions covering format/units/edge-cases lift extraction accuracy 60%+.

title: Tool Description as Prompt
one_liner: Tool descriptions ARE the prompt the model uses to pick tools and fill arguments â€” structure them as use-when / NOT-use-when / I/O contract / side-effects.

title: Prompt-Cache Prefix Order
one_liner: Order prompts stableâ†’volatile (systemâ†’toolsâ†’retrievalâ†’historyâ†’user); place cache_control after the longest stable prefix; cuts ~90% on cached portion.

title: Subagent as Context Firewall
one_liner: Use subagents to keep heavy work in isolated contexts; they return slim summary+refs only â€” parent's context grows ~500 tokens regardless of subagent's read volume.

title: Stream-JSON Orchestration
one_liner: Spawn agent CLIs (Claude Code/Codex/opencode) with --output-format stream-json so the orchestrator can react mid-run â€” budget caps, hand-offs, safety vetoes.
